101CustomIZ1：光茵荏苒:
<size=18>Chapter 1: Lawn of Time



















Welcome to I, Rigid Corpse Puzzles! Please change the difficulty to the recommended one before playing for the best experience.
Your goal is to let the zombies eat all the brains at the end of each lane. Towards Sun Flowers and Twin Towards Sun Flowers drop Sun when eaten!
Chapter Maker: MirteorShower
102CustomIZ1-1:
<size=18>Lawn of Time 1-1



















Although we're in Fusion, let's start with something simple.
Can you beat this level containing only vanilla plants and vanilla zombies?
103CustomIZ1-2:
<size=18>Lawn of Time 1-2
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 1200 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















Hopefully the difficulty spike is not so terrible?
You can check the Almanac to learn more about the zombies' abilities and traits.
N.B.: Only Towards Sun Flowers and Twin Towards Sun Flowers drop Sun when eaten.
104CustomIZ1-3教程:
<size=18>Lawn of Time 1-3 Tutorial: Sun Light Big Mushroom



















This is a tutorial level! In tutorial levels you have more than enough Sun and you can drop zombies anywhere you like!
The plant in the Pot on lane 3 is a Sun Light Big Mushroom. It absorbs Sun dropped within a 5×5 area. And it keeps it, no refunds.
When you see Sun Light Big Mushrooms, prioritize taking them out to avoid missing out on Sun!
105CustomIZ1-3:
<size=18>Lawn of Time 1-3
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 950 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















This level gives you two types of Hold Pole Rigid Corpses. The 75 Sun one is cheaper, but its HP is paper-thin.
Tip: You can toggle plant/zombie HP display on the pause menu. On PC, you can also press Q or W.
106CustomIZ1-4:
<size=18>Lawn of Time 1-4
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 1100 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















Sometimes it's just hard to stop Sun Light Big Mushroom from stealing your Sun. Don't stress - losing a little is fine. Perfection isn't required.
Btw, even though Zombotanies can attack from afar, sometimes placing them right in a plant's face works better.
Stuck? You can raise the difficulty to give zombies damage reduction. (Try not to rely on that though.)
107CustomIZ1-5-1:
<size=18>Lawn of Time 1-5-1
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 1025 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















Welcome to 1-5! It's a series of levels.
Try placing Zombotanies onto the Super Tier Machine Instrument Tough Fruits on the far right. They'll attack automatically.
Once a lane is cleared, drop a Hold Pole Rigid Corpse there to claim the brain. (You can enable fast-forward in the main menu.)
108CustomIZ1-5-2:
<size=18>Lawn of Time 1-5-2
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 1075 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















We've added an extra column of Earth Bean South Melons on the right. Now you've got room to place those defensive Zombotanies.
You can stack multiple shooter Zombotanies on the same tile for some serious firepower.
Tip: If you see a plant you don't recognize, check the Almanac. On PC, hover over it and press C for a quick lookup.
109CustomIZ1-5-3:
<size=18>Lawn of Time 1-5-3
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 900 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















Mix and match your Zombotanies wisely and flatten that plant defense line!
And don't forget, take out those Sun Light Big Mushrooms first.
Tip: Tap the Slow Mode button in the bottom-left (press 3 on PC) to slow things down and place multiple zombies more easily.
110CustomIZ1-6教程:
<size=18>Lawn of Time 1-6 Tutorial: Sun Light Squash Melon 1



















After attacking, Sun Light Squash Melon automatically consumes Sun to revive.
The more it revives the more Sun it needs. If you don't have enough Sun, it dies for good.
It's recommended to trigger its attack when your Sun is below 50 so it can't revive and won't cost anything extra.
Also, ranged attacks do NOT stop Sun Light Squash Melon from reviving. Try placing a shooter Zombotany on lane 1 to see how it works.
111CustomIZ1-6:
<size=18>Lawn of Time 1-6
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 850 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















Before baiting those Sun Light Squash Melones, try to spend as much Sun as possible.
Of course you can also choose to sacrifice some Sun to raise its revival threshold - just don't overdo it.
Tip: Remember to use Slow Mode to quickly deploy zombies when needed.
112CustomIZ1-7教程:
<size=18>Lawn of Time 1-7 Tutorial: Sun Light Squash Melon 2



















If Sun Light Squash Melon lands on a tile already occupied by a plant, it won't revive - even if you have plenty of Sun. (See lane 3.)
There are still a few Sun Light Squash Melones left on the lawn. Use the zombies in your arsenal to deal with them as efficiently as possible.
(Want to skip the tutorial? Just drop zombies in column 1 and eat the brains directly.)
113CustomIZ1-7:
<size=18>Lawn of Time 1-7
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 775 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















Even more Sun Light Squash Melones! Hope you're ready.
If you can make good use of the skill you learned in 1-7 Tutorial,
you may not need to worry about sunlight as much...
114CustomIZ1-8:
<size=18>Lawn of Time 1-8
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 925 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















Good news: This defense is made entirely of Towards Sun Flowers and their fusions.
Bad news: Both Sun Light Big Mushroom and Sun Light Squash Melones are here too.
On the bright side, you've got a new zombie - Chicken Jockey. They're fast. Really fast. Great for baiting Squash Melones.
Tip: This level has some margin for error. Even if a bit of Sun gets wasted by Sun Light Big Mushroom or Sun Light Squash Melon, you can still win.
115CustomIZ1-9:
<size=18>Lawn of Time 1-9
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 400 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















Explosive cherries from Super Tier Cherry Peach Shooter Rigid Corpses deal splash damage. Use that to hit lanes 2 and 4 in this level.
To make sure the Cherries explode in the right spot, you may need to use other zombies to help chip into the defense.
(You've seen Super Tier Cherry Peach Shooter Rigid Corpse before, but this time the position of the explosion matters a lot more.)
116CustomIZ1-10:
<size=18>Lawn of Time 1-10
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 750 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















Uh… is that a Ultra Extreme Cherry Peach Shooter in the middle lane? And it brought backup??
Ultra Extreme Cherry Peach Shooter is immune to cherry blast damage. Try using splash to clear out the shooter plants behind it first.
Once its support is gone, the lone Ultra Extreme Cherry Peach Shooter isn't so scary anymore.
201CustomIZ2：菇泛远影:
<size=18>Chapter 2: Shroomshade



















Welcome to Chapter 2! This time you'll be facing off against all kinds of mushrooms - and taking them down.
You'll also unlock new zombies with more complex mechanics. Don't forget to check the Almanac if something confuses you.
Chapter Maker: MirteorShower
202CustomIZ2-1:
<size=18>Shroomshade 2-1
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 950 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















Up to 3 Little Spray Mushrooms can stack on a single tile!
Individually their fusions might not look impressive compared to before,
but three together? That number's a problem.<size=6>
<size=12>And if they're mixed types… good luck.<size=18>
203CustomIZ2-2教程:
<size=18>Shroomshade 2-2 Tutorial: Little Courage Mushroom



















Many Little Courage Mushrooms have powerful ranged attacks. But if a zombie gets within a 3×3 area around them,
they panic and stop attacking. To exploit this, place a zombie in an adjacent lane first to make them hide, then deploy
zombies in their own lane. (See lanes 1 and 2.) This greatly reduces incoming damage and makes breaking through much easier.
204CustomIZ2-2:
<size=18>Shroomshade 2-2
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 825 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















Rigid Corpse Horse Ride Officer moves fast and can instantly trample most plants it charges into, ignoring their HP. The catch?
It's not too bulky and it needs time to start moving again after a collision. Make sure it doesn't die before its attack connects.
Tip: Some Little Courage Mushrooms attack in unusual ways. Check the Almanac for details.
205CustomIZ2-3:
<size=18>Shroomshade 2-3
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 750 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















There's an extra lane now! You'll need to eat all 6 brains to win.
A new mushroom appears: Tough Fruit Big Mushroom. It picks up nearby Tough Fruit Little Mushrooms and fire them as high-damage shots.
They are sitting ducks if no Tough Fruit Little Mushrooms are nearby. Taking out the little ones first might be the smart move.
206CustomIZ2-4:
<size=18>Shroomshade 2-4<size=12>
<alpha=#60>(Level made by 43056721, edited by MirteorShower)
<alpha=#04>Best Solution: 800 Sun left
<alpha=#00>.<alpha=#FF><size=18>

















If there are too many Tough Fruit Little Mushrooms, you can actually turn Tough Fruit Big Mushroom's mechanic against them.
Try placing a Diamond Rock Matryoshka Rigid Corpse in front of a Tough Fruit Big Mushroom to quickly clear out the annoying Tough Fruit Little Mushrooms.
<size=6>
<size=15>(Downside: you'll have to sacrifice a Diamond Rock Matryoshka Rigid Corpse for it… unless you figure out a smarter way to bait out those Tough Fruit Big Mushrooms).<size=18>
207CustomIZ2-5-1:
<size=18>Shroomshade 2-5-1
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 900 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=14><alpha=#FF>

















Welcome to 2-5. In these stages, you'll only have Football Knight Captain as your zombie. It's powerful across the board.
But here's the catch: he doesn't eat brains while mounting. You need plants to defeat the horse
and summon Giga Buckshot Commando before you can actually get the brain.
Still confused? Try placing an him in lanes 1, 3, and 5 and watch what happens.
(Remember to manually use and upgrade the Destroy Eradicate Big Spray Mushroom with Damage Upgrade.)
208CustomIZ2-5-2:
<size=18>Shroomshade 2-5-2
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 550 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















Nice work. You've probably noticed that some plants are TD plants and can be manually upgraded with Sun to boost their stats.
Upgrading is essential here. Without enough damage to knock down the Knight Captain, you won't clear the level.
<u>From now on, all upgradable plants are marked with a Sun Light Flower Pot.</u> Choose wisely and which upgrade you need.
209CustomIZ2-5-3:
<size=18>Shroomshade 2-5-3
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 1160 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>
















<size=14>

<size=18>Overspending Sun early on upgrades can leave you broke later, so plan carefully.
The order of attack matters a lot in this level.
The right sequence can boost plant damage efficiency - and even help you earn Sun.



















<size=9>
210CustomIZ2-6教程:
<size=18>Shroomshade 2-6 Tutorial: Destroy Eradicate Big Mouth Flower



















Destroy Eradicate Big Mouth Flower destroys every plant directly in front of it when it attacks. Doesn't matter what or how many - all gone instantly.
You can deliberately feed it a zombie to trigger its attack and let it "betray" its own side for your gain.
(Don't drop multiple zombies at once if you plan on using it, though.
Destroy Eradicate Mushroom explosions will also instantly wipe out most zombies.)
211CustomIZ2-6:
<size=18>Shroomshade 2-6<size=12>
<alpha=#60>(Level made by 43056721, edited by MirteorShower)
<alpha=#04>Best Solution: 800 Sun left
<alpha=#00>.<alpha=#FF><size=18>

















The Destroy Eradicate Mushroom family may be short-lived, but they unleash overwhelming power in that brief moment.
Plants have stabilized this energy through fusion, but it's still volatile.
Make clever use of Destroy Eradicate Big Mouth Flower's destructive power and blast your way to victory.
212CustomIZ2-7教程:
<size=18>Shroomshade 2-7 Tutorial: Destroy Eradicate Squash Melon



















When Destroy Eradicate Squash Melon lands, it triggers a Destroy Eradicate Mushroom explosion at the impact tile.
The blast also instantly destroys plants. (See lane 1.) Unlike Destroy Eradicate Big Mouth Flower, Destroy Eradicate Squash Melon's landing spot can be controlled,
making its explosions much more flexible. Now place zombies in column 6 of lanes 3, 4, 5, and 6 in the order
shown in your arsenal. Destroy Eradicate Squash Melon will automatically blow up those Fire Flame Tall Tough Fruits.
213CustomIZ2-7:
<size=18>Shroomshade 2-7
<size=12><alpha=#02>Best Solution: 700 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















New zombie unlocked: Olympic Pole Vaulter. It jumps extremely far and, with enough run-up, can even ignore Tall Tough Fruits.
If you want it to clear a Tall Tough Fruit, place it farther to the right to give it enough runway.
Note: The run-up only determines whether it can vault over a Tall Tough Fruit. It doesn’t change the jump distance itself.
214CustomIZ2-8教程:
<size=18>Shroomshade 2-8 Tutorial: Destroy Eradicate Charm Confuse Mushroom & Charm Confuse South Melon
<size=12><alpha=#40>Place zombies in the leftmost column to skip this tutorial.
<alpha=#FF><size=0>
<size=18>

















When a zombie eats a Destroy Eradicate Charm Confuse Mushroom, it turns into a hypnotized zombie that explodes on death, which also destroys plants. (See lane 2.)
First, send a zombie to eat the Destroy Eradicate Charm Confuse Mushroom. When the hypnotized zombie reaches the Charm Confuse South Melon, it'll be stuck in place.
Then send another zombie to kill it and trigger the explosion to destroy plants.
There's another Destroy Eradicate Charm Confuse Mushroom in the back of lane 5. Can you use it to blow up the Fire Flame Tough Fruit in column 5?



















<size=10>
215CustomIZ2-8:
<size=18>Shroomshade 2-8<size=12>
<alpha=#60>(Level made by 43056721, edited by MirteorShower)
<alpha=#04>Best Solution: 625 Sun left
<alpha=#00>.<alpha=#FF><size=18>

















A chaotic combo of various Doom fusions! Use a little <alpha=#80><s>friendly fire</s><alpha=#FF> wisely and things get much easier.
You also have Cold Ice Tall Tough Fruit Rigid Corpses here. They're immune to Chill, so ice plants are a piece of cake for them.
Tip: Not sure how to start? Try dropping a Football Knight Captain in lane 3.
216CustomIZ2-9:
<size=18>Shroomshade 2-9
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 475 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















Dance King Hold Pole Rigid Corpse summons four backup dancer vaulters around itself after jumping.
Strong mobility and summoning power - just a bit pricey.
To help you learn it faster, we've marked the lawn with many <u>Flow Light Flower Pots.</u>
Try making the Dance King Hold Pole Rigid Corpse jump over the plants on those pots. If you get the order right, this level is quickly solved.
217CustomIZ2-10:
<size=18>Shroomshade 2-10
<size=12><alpha=#02>Best Solution: 625 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















Odyssey plants are back - and there are almost twice as many this time.
Your zombies can't brute-force this. You'll need to outplay them by cleverly abusing the Doom fusions.
Like 2-9, this level also has Flow Light Flower Pot hints... but their placement seems completely meaningless...?
<i>' Shroomshade - where light reveals form.'   - Dave Tzu, perhaps
<size=6>
<size=18>
301CustomIZ3：推波助澜:
<size=18>Chapter 3: Making Waves
<size=9>
<size=18>

















The plants seem to have abandoned the lawn and moved their defenses to the pool.
Most zombies can't be placed on water lanes, but Browncoats, Coneheads, and Iron Bucketheads ignore terrain restrictions.
(Soon you'll unlock some zombies built specifically for aquatic combat.)
Chapter Maker: MirteorShower



















<size=9>
302CustomIZ3-1:
<size=18>Making Waves 3-1<size=12>
<alpha=#60>
<alpha=#04>Best Solution: 850 Sun left
<alpha=#00>(Level made by 43056721, edited by MirteorShower)<alpha=#FF><size=18>

















Snorkels can't be targeted by straight-firing plants while underwater. It's perfect for long-distance water infiltration.
It's also a great counter to Squash Melon Tough Fruit. Try dropping one directly in lane 4 and see what happens.
Tip: Squash Melon Tough Fruits might still hit Snorkels on fast-forward. If that happens, switch back to normal speed.
303CustomIZ3-2教程:
<size=18>Making Waves 3-2 Tutorial: Fire Red Lily
<size=12>Place zombies in the leftmost column to skip this tutorial.
<alpha=#FF><size=6>
<size=18>

















Fire Red Lily dies and drops a bag of Fertilizer after absorbing 6 projectiles or when its HP drops to 0. (In Puzzle Mode, it won't drop Sun.)
Even though you're playing as zombies, smart Fertilizer usage can help dismantle plant setups. For example:
<u>Big Spray Mushroom + Fertilizer = Worry Gloom Mushroom (reduced range).</u>
<u>Singe Black 3 Line Shooter + Fertilizer = normal 3 Line Shooter (halved damage).</u>



















<size=18>
304CustomIZ3-2:
<size=18>Making Waves 3-2<size=12>
<alpha=#60>
<alpha=#04>Best Solution: 775 Sun left
<alpha=#00>>(Level made by 43056721, edited by MirteorShower)<alpha=#FF><size=18>

















Squash Melon Rigid Corpse ignores HP and crushes most plants instantly. Tough, high-HP targets? Just let Squash Melon handle it.
You learned some basic Fertilizer tricks in the tutorial, but it can also do things like speed up Big Mouth Flower digestion.
Though… why would you want Big Mouth Flower to digest faster? Wouldn't slower be better for us?
305CustomIZ3-3教程:
<size=18>Making Waves 3-3 Tutorial: Cat Melon
<size=12>Place zombies in the leftmost column to skip this tutorial.
<alpha=#FF><size=6>
<size=18>

















Cat Melon drops Squash Melon seed packets based on how many zombies she crushes.
You can fuse certain plants with these Squash Melones to "nerf" them. For example:
<u>Tough Fruit + Squash Melon = Squash Melon Tough Fruit (leakier defense).</u>   <u>Charm Confuse Mushroom + Squash Melon = Charm Confuse Squash Melon (juke-able).</u>
For comparison, place a Olive Tall Tough Fruit Rigid Corpse in column 6 of lane 1 and lane 2.
The one in lane 2 survives and eats the brain. The one in lane 1 does not.



















<size=18>
306CustomIZ3-3:
<size=18>Making Waves 3-3<size=12>
<alpha=#60>(Level made by 树桩上的火炎, edited by MirteorShower)
<alpha=#04>Best Solution: 850 Sun left
<alpha=#00>.<alpha=#FF><size=18>

















<size=9>
<size=18>White Whale Ride Officer can only be placed in water lanes but vaults an extremely
long distance (4 tiles!) and then turn around to attack plants from behind.
Unsure with which plant to fuse your Squash Melones? Consider the vanilla plants on the field. (Except Towards Sun Flowers)




















<size=9>
307CustomIZ3-4:
<size=18>Making Waves 3-4
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 800 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















Fire Red Lily not only provides fertilizer, but can also absorb plant projectiles, reducing the damage zombies take.
In this level, some fertilizer might be redundant, but properly using Fire Red Lily to sponge damage can also help you clear the stage!
Tip: Turn on Plant HP display to check how many more projectiles Fire Red Lily can absorb (blue number).<size=18>
308CustomIZ3-5-1:
<size=18>Making Waves 3-5-1
<size=12><alpha=#00>Best Solution: 950 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















The zombies think you're playing terribly! It looks like they've decided to rely on themselves.
Do nothing. Win. (yes, the next two levels are self-playing stages as well).
Still, we've prepared some backup units at your disposal, just in case something goes wrong...
or if you get bored watching and want to skip ahead.<size=18>
309CustomIZ3-5-2:
<size=18>Making Waves 3-5-2<size=12>
<alpha=#60>(Level made by MirteorShower制作)
<alpha=#00>Best Solution: 0 Sun left
<alpha=#00>.<alpha=#FF><size=18>
Watch this zombie closely →<alpha=#00>-----------------------------------
<alpha=#FF>He's attempting the challenge to eat 5 brains at once. Can he pull it off?<alpha=#00>---------------------------------------------















<alpha=#FF><size=16>Basic Info: Auto stage; fast-forward allowed; Minor plant stacking
Testing shows said Rigid Corpse only has about a 70% chance of eating all 5 brains, due to animation variance between zombies and plants (unavoidable).
If he fails to eat all 5 brains, use the Gargantuar in your seed slots to deal with any remaining water-lane,
or simply retry the level and give him another chance.
<size=18>
310CustomIZ3-5-3:
<size=18>Making Waves 3-5-3<size=12>
<alpha=#60>(Level made by 43056721)
<alpha=#00>Best Solution: 800 Sun left
<alpha=#00>.<alpha=#FF><size=18>

















Basic Info: Auto stage; fast-forward not supported; 99% clearance rate at normal speed
May fail to auto-clear (14% success rate) if fast-forward is enabled.
Pick your poison. (full run is approximately 7 mins long).
Tip: If you feel the wait is too long, you can place zombies manually to skip the level.
311CustomIZ3-6教程:
<size=18>Making Waves 3-6 Tutorial: Cryo & Pyro Infusions



















Cryo/Fire Flame Cannon Artillerys fuse plants affected by the blast with Cold Ice Mushroom or Fire Burst Spicy Pepper. Use this to weaken the plant setup:
<u>Little Spray Mushroom + Cold Ice Mushroom = Cold Ice Little Mushroom/<u> <u>3 Line Shooter + 2 Fire Burst Spicy Peppers = Ash Ember 3 Line Shooter</u>
(Many more examples exist, omitted due to space)
<alpha=#FF>Lanes 4 and 5 are a mini-test. If fused correctly, only 4 Browncoats are needed in total for these 2 lanes.
312CustomIZ3-6:
<size=18>Making Waves 3-6
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 800 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















Think twice before blasting plants with the Fire Flame Cannon Artillery - most plants actually get stronger after fusing with Fire Burst Spicy Pepper.
This level also features the Hydrofowl. It ignores HP and crush one water-lane plant, then launch an Little Devil Rigid Corpse onto nearby land.
Tip: Tap the far-left side of the screen to cancel cannon aiming.<size=18>
313CustomIZ3-7教程:
<size=18>Making Waves 3-7 Tutorial: Ice-Fire Conflict



















Ice and fire plants are both powerful - but combine them and they'll cancel each other out.
Lane 1's Flame Big Spray Mushroom looks stronger than Lane 2's normal one, but due to ice-fire conflict, it actually deals less total damage.
<alpha=#FF>Mini test for Lanes 4 and 5: fuse correctly and you'll only need <u>2 Olive Ball Rigid Corpses total</u> to eat both brains.
314CustomIZ3-7:
<size=18>Making Waves 3-7
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 750 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa

<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















When shooter Zombotanies are chilled, their movement speed is halved - but their attack speed stays the same.
If you find your Zombotanies are walking too fast, try chilling them out with some ice.
<size=18>


















<size=6>
315CustomIZ3-8:
<size=18>Making Waves 3-8<size=12>
<alpha=#60>(Level made by 树桩上的火炎, edited by MirteorShower)
<alpha=#04>Best Solution: 625 Sun left
<alpha=#00>.<alpha=#FF><size=18>

















Fire Burst Squash Melon Rigid Corpse can instantly wipe an entire lane, as long as it isn't chilled. (See the Almanac for details)
<alpha=#FF>Keep it warm. Use fertilizer and ice-fire conflict to prevent it from getting chilled.
Tip: Bugs may occur when using the Fire Burst Squash Melon Rigid Corpse along with a higher game speed. If you are using the Fire Burst Squash Melon Rigid Corpse, please do not try to enable higher game speeds...
316CustomIZ3-9:
<size=18>Making Waves 3-9
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 475 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















Alright, now the plants are serious. They've planted tons of Magic Way Rabbit Ear Leeks along both sides of the road.
Your zombies can't survive many zaps - but only their "captain" can attack, with a 5×5 range. So…
Tap a Magic Way Rabbit Ear Leek to make it Captain. They only attack zombies within the Captain's 5×5 area.</u><size=18>
317CustomIZ3-10:
<size=18>Making Waves 3-10
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 600 Sun left
<size=6><alpha=#00>awa
<size=18><alpha=#FF>

















Uh... Something's not right. Were the Magic Way Rabbit Ear Leeks arranged like this last level?
No matter who you pick as captain, their attack range always covers Lanes 2 and 5. That shouldn't be possible...
<i>Break the rules.  Fuse to win.   - Dave Tzu, perhaps<size=18>
401CustomIZ4：磁荧流转:
<size=18>Chapter 4: Polarity Shift



















Welcome to the fog chapter! Plant formations will become even more complex, so exercise prudence!
This chapter also introduces some more flexible new zombie mechanics.
You'll gradually discover and learn to use them in later levels. Good luck!
402CustomIZ4-1教程:
<size=18>Polarity Shift  4-1 Tutorial: Lumos System
<size=335>
<size=18>The Lumos System is a key mechanic in Fusion. Many Lumos plants gain bonuses from "Lumos Levels":
Plants in Lane 6 are greatly enhanced by Lumos Level, but become very weak without it.
Lined up in Lane 1 are some plants that provide Lumos Levels. In actual play, prioritize targeting them first.
<alpha=#30><size=12>(Check the Almanac for exact details)
<size=350>
403CustomIZ4-1:
<size=18>Polarity Shift  4-1
<size=12><alpha=#03>Best Solution: 825 Sun left
<size=330><alpha=#00>
<size=18><alpha=#FF>One more thing: starting from this chapter, plant setups will include South Melons & flying plants.
…But there's nothing to worry about. Some South Melon fusions may look flashy, but they're just for show.
<size=18>For example, in this level, those colorful South Melons behave just like the good ol' South Melon.
<size=360>
404CustomIZ4-2教程:
<size=18>Polarity Shift  4-2 Tutorial: Air Ball Rigid Corpse



















Air Ball Rigid Corpses can ignore most plants and fly right over them, reaching the house unimpeded if not countered (see Lane 1)
However, anti-air plants like Sage Person Palm can still attack them. Regular balloons pop with a single hit (see Lanes 2-4)
Besides Cacti, some other plants can also damage Air Ball Rigid Corpses (e.g. Cold Ice Little Courage Mushroom in Lane 5, Fire Torch Ground Spike in Lane 6)
405CustomIZ4-2:
<size=18>Polarity Shift  4-2<size=12>
<alpha=#60>(Level made by help from 小董玩的好)
<alpha=#04>Best Solution: 750 Sun left
<alpha=#FF><size=330>
<size=18>Iron Bucket Air Ball Rigid Corpses are a Air Ball Rigid Corpse variant that can resist some anti-air attacks without being popped.
However, they can still be intercepted by Sage Person Palm Tough Fruits. Although, getting shot down midway isn't always a bad thing.<alpha=#FF>
<size=370>
406CustomIZ4-3教程:
<size=18>Polarity Shift  4-3 Tutorial: Lantern Cage Big Mouth Flower</color>
<size=22>
<size=72><alpha=#00>iii<alpha=#10><mark=#00FF00><alpha=#00>iiiiiiiiii</mark><alpha=#10><mark=#FFFF00><alpha=#00>iiiiiiiiiiii</mark>
<size=18>iiiiiiiiiiii<alpha=#FF>|<--------Lantern Cage Big Mouth Flower Attraction Range-------->|
<size=18>
Blindspot for Attracted Rigid Corpses┐<alpha=#00>iiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiii
<alpha=#FF>|<---------┴--------->|<alpha=#00>iiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiii
<size=72><alpha=#00>iii<alpha=#10><mark=#00FF00><alpha=#00>iiiiiiiiii</mark><alpha=#10><mark=#FFFF00><alpha=#00>iiiiiiiiiiii
<size=98>
</mark><size=18><alpha=#FF>Meet Lantern Cage Big Mouth Flower. You've probably seen it already. After finishing digestion, it attracts a zombie from adjacent lanes into its own.
You can feed zombies to it to cause a lane switching, letting another zombie bypass strong defenses and strike the backline.
Try using lane-switching to move the Yellow Gold Matryoshka Rigid Corpse from Lane 6 to Lane 4 and eat the brain!
<size=12><alpha=#40>(PS: You don't need to time the digestion precisely. In later levels, lane switching will always come assisted by Tough Fruits in adjacent lanes)
<size=350>
407CustomIZ4-3:
<size=18>Polarity Shift  4-3<size=12>
<alpha=#60>(Level made by help from 小董玩的好, edited by MirteorShower)
<alpha=#04>Best Solution: 725 Sun left
<alpha=#FF><size=322>
<size=18>Fly Ship Rigid Corpses are powerful flying units with high HP that can bomb an entire lane.
Their downside is their high cost and inability to eat brains, so they're best used only when truly needed.<alpha=#FF>
<size=16>Tip: When using Lantern Cage Big Mouth Flower's lane-switching, make sure its lane is empty (if zombies are already present, switching won't trigger).
<size=362>
408CustomIZ4-4:
<size=18>Polarity Shift  4-4<size=12>
<alpha=#60>(Level made by help from decepticons, edited by MirteorShower)
<alpha=#04>Best Solution: 650 Sun left
<alpha=#FF><size=322>
<size=16>You've unlocked Mine Worker Rigid Corpse! Don't worry, you won't need to perfect time their surfacing with Magnet Force Mushrooms to clear every level.
Mine Worker Rigid Corpses dig underground to the backline and sneak attack from behind if uninterrupted.
If intercepted mid-way by Magnet Force Mushrooms or Mine Worker South Melons, they surface and attack normally.
	Tip: If boosted by Lumos, Flow Light Magnet Force Mushrooms gain a huge attraction range. Without Lumos, their range (1.2 tiles) is barely half of a normal Magnet Force Mushroom.<alpha=#FF>
<size=362>

412CustomIZ4-6教程:
<size=18>Polarity Shift  4-6 Tutorial: Magnet Force South Melon & Magnetic Links
<size=85>
<size=15><align=left><alpha=#00>iiiiiiiiii<alpha=#FF><size=60%>e.g. Damage multiplier of this Iron Bucket South Melon ↓</size>
<alpha=#00>iiiiiiiiii<alpha=#FF>  10 (num of linked magnet plants) ×
<alpha=#00>iiiiiiiiii<alpha=#FF>2 (num of nearby Magnet Force South Melons) ×
<alpha=#00>iiiiiiiiii<alpha=#FF><u>× 2 (in water) = 40 (Damage multiplier)</u>
<alpha=#45>Damage: ×1.3(Iron Iron Bucket speed) ×20 =1040
<size=72><alpha=#00>iiiiiiiiiiiiii<alpha=#60><mark=#FFFF00><alpha=#00>iiii</mark>
<size=98>
<align=center><size=16><alpha=#FF>Every 6s, Magnet Force South Melons deal AoE damage to nearby (3×3) <u>metal zombies*</u>. The more magnet plants linked, the higher the <u>damage*</u></u>.
So yeah - cut the link first to reduce damage, or just use non-metal zombies and ignore the whole thing.
Further fusions also inherit this mechanic, and multiple Magnet Force South Melons can stack damage (it adds up fast).
<size=9><align=left><alpha=#60>
*Metal zombies: all zombies in the first row of the card bar count, plus Snow Sled Vehicle Rigid Corpses (yes, really, but who even uses Magnet Force South Melon on them lol)
<alpha=#60>Check the almanac for the calculation formula. Do not worry about it too much.
<size=345>
413CustomIZ4-6:
<size=18>Polarity Shift  4-6<size=12>
<alpha=#60>(Level made by help from 树桩上的火炎, edited by MirteorShower)
<alpha=#03>Best Solution: 700 Sun left
<alpha=#FF><size=320>
<size=18>Jump Jump Rigid Corpses can jump over most plants, but they also just hop off the lawn instead of eating the brains.
If blocked by Tall Tough Fruits or disarmed by Magnet Force Mushrooms, they'll be knocked down and start attacking normally
(and yes, they can eat brains then).
Tip: Breaking magnetic links can massively reduce Magnet Force South Melon's damage!<alpha=#FF>
<size=360>
414CustomIZ4-7教程:
<size=18>Polarity Shift  4-7 Tutorial: Metal Objects & Magnet Force Destroy Eradicate Mushroom
<size=12>
<size=18>

















Magnet Force Destroy Eradicate Mushroom does not explode by default, but once fused with <u>specific metal items*</u>, it will detonate and destroy all plants on its tile.
You can obtain metal objects via Magnet Force Mushrooms, and most metal Zombotanies will also drop them upon defeat.
Drag a metal item onto the Magnet Force Destroy Eradicate Mushroom to set it off - great for clearing Drones, South Melons, or breaking Magnetic links.
<size=12><alpha=#30>
*Specific metal objects: currently Iron Bucket, Jack-in-the-Box, and Chrono Cores. The latter two are… let's just say rare, so most future levels stick to Iron Bucket fusions.

<size=18>
415CustomIZ4-7:
<size=18>Polarity Shift  4-7<size=12>
<alpha=#60>(Level made with help from 小董玩得好, edited by MirteorShower)
<alpha=#04>Best Solution: 650 Sun left
<alpha=#FF><size=320>
<size=18>Buck-shooter zombies won't get insta-killed by Magnet Force Mushrooms but they still take damage from Magnet Force South Melons.
Also, Buckshoot-copter zombies can be deployed over water. Sure, they'll disappear eventually,
but not before doing some serious damage to the frontline.
Tip: Rigid Corpses killed by Iron Bucket Destroy Eradicate Mushroom drop more Iron Iron Buckets.<alpha=#FF>
<size=360>
416CustomIZ4-8:
<size=18>Polarity Shift  4-8<size=12>
<alpha=#60>(Level made with help from decepticons)
<alpha=#04>Best Solution: 500 Sun left
<alpha=#FF><size=320>
<size=18>Super Tier South Melons come with airborne wingmen support that targets the whole lawn, but Bucknut-copter zombies can still tank hits for a while before going down.
Use this window to set up early landings and dismantle the plant backline.
<alpha=#70>Tip: If you're stuck at the start, maybe check out Flow Light Foreign Peach's Almanac entry… and try cutting its Lumos supply a little.
<size=360>
417CustomIZ4-9: Polarity Shift 4-9 (Level made with help from 43056721, later edits by MirteorShower) Best Solution: 475 Sun left You found a note! 418CustomIZ4-10教程:
<size=18>Polarity Shift  4-10 Tutorial: Hypnot Magnet & Object Feeding<size=12>
<alpha=#00>Level maker: decepticons         Best Solution: 400 Sun left</color>
<alpha=#00>Best Solution: 800 Sun left
<alpha=#00>.<alpha=#FF><size=18>
<size=300>
<size=18>When any Magnet Force Mushroom (except Magnet Force Mushroom King) finishes consumption, it transfers the metal object to
a <color=#F2B6DB>Charm Confuse Magnet Force Mushroom</color> of the same magnetic system. <color=#F2B6DB>Charm Confuse Magnet Force Mushroom</color>can use these metals to produce hypnotized zombies. 
When paired with Charm Confuse Big Mouth Flower, they can make <color=yellow>Charm Confuse Mushrooms</color> seed packets for you. (see lane 1)
<size=12>Note: If both <color=#F2B6DB>Charm Confuse Magnet Force Mushroom</color> and <color=#DD96E5>Ultra Extreme Charm Confuse Mushroom</color> exist in the same system, metals will always go to <color=#DD96E5>Ultra Extreme Charm Confuse Mushroom</color> first. (See lanes 4,5,6)
Feeding objects to <color=#DD96E5>Ultra Extreme Charm Confuse Mushroom</color> does you no good, so remember to cut the link to <color=#DD96E5>Ultra Extreme Charm Confuse Mushroom</color> to avoid wasting objects.
<size=350>
419CustomIZ4-10:
<size=18>Polarity Shift  4-10<size=12></color>
<alpha=#03>Best Solution: 500 Sun left
<alpha=#00>Best Solution: 700 Sun left
<alpha=#FF><size=320>
<size=16>Final fog stage! But first - you'll need to deal with the <color=#61AB3D>Ultra Extreme Sea Demon Mushroom</color>, easily one of the nastiest encounters so far.
It periodically summons minions to drag a zombie underwater. You can stall it with fodder, but that's not exactly a long-term strategy.
Rumor has it that its <color=#DA8EBA>Charm Confuse Mushroom form</color> weakens it significantly.
So if you can get your hands on a <color=#DA8EBA>Charm Confuse Mushroom</color> seed packet, things might just turn around.<alpha=#FF>
<size=360>

602CustomIZ3-10提示: 3-10 Hint (This is a hint for 3-10, so feel free to skip if you've already cleared it!) Best Solution: 700 Sun left You can fuse a fifth Magic Way Rabbit Ear Leek outside the formation using Fertilizer and seed packets, then set it as leader to disable the ones inside the formation from attacking. Fusion Roadmap: [Sleep Lily + Fertilizer → Cat Tail Grass] [Cat Tail Grass + Cat Melon → Magic Way Cat Tail Grass] [Magic Way Cat Tail Grass + Surprise Happy Gift Box → Magic Way Rabbit Ear Leek] Cat Melon: Make the Cat Melon in lane 6 miss its target by activating Destroy Eradicate Big Spray Mushroom Surprise Happy Gift Box: Feed Fertilizer to a Rigid Corpse Fire Red Lily

[Magnet Force Foreign Peach + Pickaxe → Mine Worker Foreign Peach]: no longer blocks zombies
[Magnet Force South Melon + Pickaxe → Mine Worker South Melon]: forces Diggers to surface

901CustomIZ原汁原味:
Welcome to I, Rigid Corpse puzzlers! Please follow the recommended difficulties, or it might lead to impossible levels or cheese!
This first stage is very easy, and levels are arranged from easier to harder.
Please make use of Snow Sled Vehicle Rigid Corpse's chill immunity and Pole Vaulters to clear the level~
